mnemonics: Geth Alone! (Geth is the most common 'go-ethereum' client run alone by many users and developers)
keyPoints: The two main types of clients are Execution Clients (like Geth, Nethermind) and Consensus Clients (like Lighthouse, Prysm).
Releases are categorized by hard forks (backwards-incompatible upgrades) with codenames like London, Paris, and Shanghai.
Clients must be updated to stay in sync with the network protocol after a hard fork.
commonMistakes: Thinking all Ethereum software is just 'Geth'. There are many client implementations in different languages.
Believing a hard fork is a 'split' or a negative event. On Ethereum, they are planned, coordinated network upgrades.
Not updating a client after a hard fork, which will cause it to fall out of sync and onto the wrong chain.
